statement.markdown: Given a string, write a function to trim the string by removing leading and trailing whitespaces. However, the function contains a logical bug. Fix the code to pass all test cases.
statement.inputFormat: A string may contain leading/trailing whitespaces, or no whitespaces at all.
statement.outputFormat: The trimmed string without any leading/trailing whitespaces.
statement.notes: Time complexity should be O(n), where n is the length of the input string.

examples.explanation: Trim the leading/trailing whitespaces in the input string

hints.text: Use the strip() method or a loop with slicing to trim the string

editorial.title: Understanding String Trimming
editorial.intuition: The goal is to remove leading and trailing whitespaces from the input string
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach involves iterating through the string and checking each character
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach uses the strip() method or a loop with slicing
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution should have a time complexity of O(n), where n is the length of the input string
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Test with different input strings to ensure the function works correctly
editorial.dryRun: Test the function with a short input string to see the output
commonMistakes: Ignoring the case of input strings

starterCode.python: class Solution: def trim(self, s: str) -> str: pass # TODO: implement

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: string trim(string s) { if (s.length() == 0) return s; return s.substr(1); } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public String trim(String s) { if (s.length() == 0) return s; return s.substring(1); } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def trim(self, s: str) -> str: if not s: return s return s[1:];
buggyStarterCode.javascript: function trim(s) { if (s.length == 0) return s; return s.substring(1); }
